cover_tagline: One WhatsApp number, one AI, one brain. You text or voice-note anything and it sorts itself.
primary_problem: You're juggling people, properties, follow-ups, research, and HubSpot busywork across too many tools. The cost isn't time so much as cognitive load: ideas dropped in the car, follow-ups slipping past their due date, twenty minutes of HubSpot setup every time you want to run a sequence, and a research backlog that never quite gets answered.
desired_outcome: One WhatsApp number, one AI, one brain. You text or voice-note anything (random thoughts, reminders, research requests, PDFs to process, HubSpot jobs) and it sorts itself. No bot teams. No routing mazes. No picking which tool to open.
current_tools: WhatsApp, HubSpot, Google Calendar, Gmail, Fireflies, Google Drive
trigger_description: You text or voice-note one WhatsApp number, any time, from anywhere. We register a new dedicated number for the assistant, so it appears as its own contact in your phone.
process_description: The brain reads or transcribes your message, figures out intent (capture, reminder, research, HubSpot job, pre-meeting brief), pulls memory of relevant people and properties, and picks the right tool from its toolbox.
output_description: The answer comes back in WhatsApp. Reminders fire on time with the why attached. Memos arrive when ready. Briefs return in seconds. HubSpot jobs are prepared and shown to you before they run.
notification_description: Anything that sends a message, creates a record, or touches HubSpot waits for your explicit yes. No outbound action without confirmation, ever.

scope_items.label: WhatsApp inbound on a new dedicated number (text + voice notes), set up via Whapi
Voice transcription via Groq Whisper, sub-second on most messages
Single Claude-based brain with tool routing. No bot teams, no routing mazes.
Private memory database in Supabase: people, properties, ideas, follow-ups, captures, research memos. Yours, exportable any time.
Capture skill: any text or voice note auto-filed under the right person, property, idea, or follow-up. Searchable on demand.
Pre-meeting brief: text 'walking to Evan' and get back 5 to 15 bullet points in seconds. Pulls open follow-ups, recent deal activity, last meeting notes.
Reminders engine: timed reminders that include the why, not just the what. Example: 'Call Peter, Drummondville papers (waiting 9 days).'
Waiting-on tracker: live list of every chase, weekly Monday digest of overdue items, auto-drafted follow-up messages, and a tap-to-confirm prompt when something arrives in your inbox or HubSpot.
Daily morning digest: 7 AM brief with today's meetings (one-line context each), overdue chases, research ready, and yesterday's captures you might have forgotten.

Research skill: topic in (text or voice), structured memo out within 1 to 6 hours. Memo includes summary, key facts, opportunities, risks, sources. Handles Section 8 grants, density-arbitrage candidates, airport infrastructure, Guyana intel, prediction markets, government bidding.
Document parsing: contract PDF returns key-terms summary; rent roll PDF returns proforma in a Google Sheet; broker contact PDF returns clean structured contacts ready for HubSpot.
HubSpot chat-ops: paste contacts and the assistant prepares the import + tags + sequence queue; you reply yes and it runs.
Calendar integration for daily digest and meeting context (Google Calendar)
Fireflies integration so meeting transcripts feed pre-meeting briefs and waiting-on items
Gmail integration so the brain detects when a chased reply lands and prompts you to close the item
Approval gate on every outbound action. No message sends, no spend, no record changes without your yes.
Database export endpoint so you can pull all your data any time, any format
Same stack as the Woodland GEM engine (Claude + Supabase + n8n) so future glue to GEM data is days, not a rewrite
Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach (Zoom/Meet auto-join, real-time hints, post-call summary)
Owner identification on Quebec property data (redacted by provincial privacy law)
Real-time listing-change alerts (we poll daily, not real-time)

out_of_scope: Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach is a separate add-on. The assistant will not send messages, spend money, or modify records without your approval. Owner identification on Montreal property data is blocked at the source by Quebec privacy law. Procurement of a new WhatsApp SIM is on you, but we register and configure it via Whapi during week 1.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.deliverable: Dedicated WhatsApp number registered via Whapi, Claude brain wired up on Railway, Supabase memory deployed, voice transcription live, capture skill working end-to-end so text and voice both file under the right person or property.

phases.name: Skills + Tools
phases.deliverable: Pre-meeting brief, reminders, waiting-on tracker, daily morning digest, research skill (all 6 topic types from your brief), document parser, HubSpot chat-ops, and Calendar + Gmail + Fireflies integrations all online and tested.

phases.name: Polish + Handoff
phases.deliverable: Approval gates verified across every outbound action, edge cases ironed out, full walkthrough on a live call, written reference card for how to talk to the assistant. 30-day support window begins.

pricing_model_details: Fixed fee, all-inclusive build via Upwork. Covers design, build, testing, walkthrough, and 30 days of post-launch support. Ongoing tool costs (listed below) are direct provider charges, not billed by me.
support_period: 30 days post-delivery, including bug fixes, prompt tuning, and small adjustments
monthly_recurring_costs: Estimated $165 to $260 per month at typical usage. Whapi (WhatsApp infrastructure) ~$30 to $50. Claude API (the brain) ~$20 to $80. Groq Whisper (voice transcription) ~$5 to $15. Apify (Centris listing detail + realtor.ca) ~$40. ZenRows (Cloudflare bypass for property research) $69. Supabase (memory database) typically $0 to $25. All other research APIs (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold, Données Québec, CanadaBuys, Montreal open data, SEAO Quebec mirror) are free public APIs. Existing subscriptions you already pay for (HubSpot, Google Workspace, Fireflies) are unchanged.
roi_details: The real return is reclaimed cognitive load: nothing slips, nothing gets re-asked, follow-ups fire themselves, research backlog clears. On pure time, voice-note capture plus chat-ops HubSpot work alone returns a clear 10 to 15 hours a month. The compounding value is in deals you don't drop and ideas you don't lose.
access_requirements: Decision on the new WhatsApp number (we can buy a cheap eSIM together on the kickoff call, ~$5 to $10 a month)
HubSpot admin access or admin-level API token
Google Calendar, Gmail, and Google Drive read access (OAuth on the kickoff call)
30-minute kickoff call to map the people, properties, and follow-up patterns the brain should know on day one
Availability for 2 to 3 short check-ins during the build
upsell_opportunities: Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach (auto-joins Zoom/Meet, whispers real-time hints to a second screen, post-call summary and action items in WhatsApp). Direct read-only hook to the Woodland GEM pipeline so the morning digest includes overnight GEM moves (1 to 2 days of glue work, same stack). Specialized recurring scan modules: daily density-arbitrage monitor for a fixed target zone, daily SEAO Quebec keyword monitor for matching tenders.

parts.text:
You are a proposal QA reviewer for a freelance AI consultant. Analyze this HTML proposal for a client and check for:

1. STRUCTURAL: Missing critical sections (cover, scope/deliverables, timeline, pricing, CTA), broken HTML tags, unclosed divs
2. CONTENT: Placeholder text ([CLIENT], lorem ipsum, TBD, TODO, undefined, null), empty sections with no content, obviously duplicated paragraphs
3. PRICING: Price showing as $0 or NaN, missing currency, price in description not matching the pricing section
4. PROFESSIONAL: Client name missing or wrong, company name empty, broken email links

Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no explanation:
{"pass": true, "issues": [], "severity": "none"}
OR if issues found:
{"pass": false, "issues": ["description of issue 1"], "severity": "minor|major"}

Only FAIL for issues that would look unprofessional to a paying client.

IM
Iván Manfredi
Proposal • April 27, 2026
AI & Automation Architect

Personal AI Assistant

One WhatsApp number, one AI, one brain. You text or voice-note anything and it sorts itself.

27
APRIL 2026
Prepared ForCharles Cukier
EngagementPersonal
Delivery2 Weeks
Investment$5500
01 • The Problem

What’s costing you time

You're juggling people, properties, follow-ups, research, and HubSpot busywork across too many tools. The cost isn't time so much as cognitive load: ideas dropped in the car, follow-ups slipping past their due date, twenty minutes of HubSpot setup every time you want to run a sequence, and a research backlog that never quite gets answered.

Currently absorbing approximately 15+ hours per week in manual work, with the additional cost of dropped follow-ups and missed context.

02 • The Solution

What we’re building

One WhatsApp number, one AI, one brain. You text or voice-note anything (random thoughts, reminders, research requests, PDFs to process, HubSpot jobs) and it sorts itself. No bot teams. No routing mazes. No picking which tool to open.

Built on Claude + n8n, integrated with WhatsApp, HubSpot, Google Calendar, Gmail, Fireflies, Google Drive. Designed to remove manual touchpoints while keeping you in full control.

System Flow

01
Trigger
You text or voice-note one WhatsApp number, any time, from anywhere. We register a new dedicated number for the assistant, so it appears as its own contact in your phone.
02
Process
The brain reads or transcribes your message, figures out intent (capture, reminder, research, HubSpot job, pre-meeting brief), pulls memory of relevant people and properties, and picks the right tool from its toolbox.
03
Output
The answer comes back in WhatsApp. Reminders fire on time with the why attached. Memos arrive when ready. Briefs return in seconds. HubSpot jobs are prepared and shown to you before they run.
04
Confirm
Anything that sends a message, creates a record, or touches HubSpot waits for your explicit yes. No outbound action without confirmation, ever.
03 • Scope of Work

What’s included

WhatsApp inbound on a new dedicated number (text + voice notes), set up via Whapi
Voice transcription via Groq Whisper, sub-second on most messages
Single Claude-based brain with tool routing. No bot teams, no routing mazes.
Private memory database in Supabase: people, properties, ideas, follow-ups, captures, research memos. Yours, exportable any time.
Capture skill: any text or voice note auto-filed under the right person, property, idea, or follow-up. Searchable on demand.
Pre-meeting brief: text 'walking to Evan' and get back 5 to 15 bullet points in seconds. Pulls open follow-ups, recent deal activity, last meeting notes.
Reminders engine: timed reminders that include the why, not just the what. Example: 'Call Peter, Drummondville papers (waiting 9 days).'
Waiting-on tracker: live list of every chase, weekly Monday digest of overdue items, auto-drafted follow-up messages, and a tap-to-confirm prompt when something arrives in your inbox or HubSpot.
Daily morning digest: 7 AM brief with today's meetings (one-line context each), overdue chases, research ready, and yesterday's captures you might have forgotten.
Research skill: topic in (text or voice), structured memo out within 1 to 6 hours. Memo includes summary, key facts, opportunities, risks, sources. Handles Section 8 grants, density-arbitrage candidates, airport infrastructure, Guyana intel, prediction markets, government bidding.
Document parsing: contract PDF returns key-terms summary; rent roll PDF returns proforma in a Google Sheet; broker contact PDF returns clean structured contacts ready for HubSpot.
HubSpot chat-ops: paste contacts and the assistant prepares the import + tags + sequence queue; you reply yes and it runs.
Calendar integration for daily digest and meeting context (Google Calendar)
Fireflies integration so meeting transcripts feed pre-meeting briefs and waiting-on items
Gmail integration so the brain detects when a chased reply lands and prompts you to close the item
Approval gate on every outbound action. No message sends, no spend, no record changes without your yes.
Database export endpoint so you can pull all your data any time, any format
Same stack as the Woodland GEM engine (Claude + Supabase + n8n) so future glue to GEM data is days, not a rewrite
Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach (Zoom/Meet auto-join, real-time hints, post-call summary)
Owner identification on Quebec property data (redacted by provincial privacy law)
Real-time listing-change alerts (we poll daily, not real-time)

Not Included

Phase 2 Live Meeting Coach is a separate add-on. The assistant will not send messages, spend money, or modify records without your approval. Owner identification on Montreal property data is blocked at the source by Quebec privacy law. Procurement of a new WhatsApp SIM is on you, but we register and configure it via Whapi during week 1.

04 • Timeline

Delivery schedule

PhaseDurationDeliverable
FoundationWeek 1, Days 1 to 4Dedicated WhatsApp number registered via Whapi, Claude brain wired up on Railway, Supabase memory deployed, voice transcription live, capture skill working end-to-end so text and voice both file under the right person or property.
Skills + ToolsWeek 1 to 2, Days 5 to 10Pre-meeting brief, reminders, waiting-on tracker, daily morning digest, research skill (all 6 topic types from your brief), document parser, HubSpot chat-ops, and Calendar + Gmail + Fireflies integrations all online and tested.
Polish + HandoffWeek 2, Days 11 to 14Approval gates verified across every outbound action, edge cases ironed out, full walkthrough on a live call, written reference card for how to talk to the assistant. 30-day support window begins.
Total: 2 Weeks
05 • Investment

Pricing

$5500
Fixed fee, all-inclusive build via Upwork. Covers design, build, testing, walkthrough, and 30 days of post-launch support. Ongoing tool costs (listed below) are direct provider charges, not billed by me.
Payment TermsHandled by Upwork escrow, milestone-based
Support Included30 days post-delivery, including bug fixes, prompt tuning, and small adjustments
Start DateUpon Upwork contract acceptance
Ongoing Tool Costs (Client-Side)

Estimated $165 to $260 per month at typical usage.

  • Whapi (WhatsApp infrastructure) ~$30 to $50.
  • Claude API (the brain) ~$20 to $80.
  • Groq Whisper (voice transcription) ~$5 to $15.
  • Apify (Centris listing detail + realtor.ca) ~$40.
  • ZenRows (Cloudflare bypass for property research) $69.
  • Supabase (memory database) typically $0 to $25.
  • All other research APIs (Polymarket, Kalshi, Manifold, Données Québec, CanadaBuys, Montreal open data, SEAO Quebec mirror) are free public APIs.
  • Existing subscriptions you already pay for (HubSpot, Google Workspace, Fireflies) are unchanged.
06 • Expected ROI

What you get back

The real return is reclaimed cognitive load: nothing slips, nothing gets re-asked, follow-ups fire themselves, research backlog clears. On pure time, voice-note capture plus chat-ops HubSpot work alone returns a clear 10 to 15 hours a month. The compounding value is in deals you don't drop and ideas you don't lose.

07 • Requirements

What I need from you

  • Decision on the new WhatsApp number (we can buy a cheap eSIM together on the kickoff call, ~$5 to $10 a month)
  • HubSpot admin access or admin-level API token
  • Google Calendar, Gmail, and Google Drive read access (OAuth on the kickoff call)
  • Fireflies account access
  • 30-minute kickoff call to map the people, properties, and follow-up patterns the brain should know on day one
  • Availability for 2 to 3 short check-ins during the build

Let’s build this

Reply on Upwork to get started, or send any questions through the platform.

Future Opportunities
